reasoning: The resolution requires >10x the reference value (which translates to roughly 64 'Battles' in a 30-day period). Historically, Guatemala has seen around ~50-60 recorded ACLED 'Battles' in an entire year (translating to ~5 per 30 days). Even with recent surges in cartel violence and gang homicides pushing May 2025 to be the most violent month under the current administration, homicides are only up relatively linearly (e.g. 21% YoY). To achieve ~64 distinct armed clash events in 30 days would demand at least two distinct battles every single day for a month — an unprecedented magnitude of organized clash density for Guatemala that borders on full-scale civil war. Without ongoing massive military offensives or a complete collapse of state control, reaching this 10x multiplier in any of the targeted monthly windows is extremely unlikely. Per the rules, I am increasing uncertainty slightly for more distant dates, starting near the absolute minimum allowed probability.
